brief:
# UX Research Brief: CashPulse Dashboard — Savings Goals Card Addition

**Document Type:** UX Research & Design Brief
**Screen:** Dashboard (Screen 1)
**Feature:** Savings Goals Card
**Version:** 1.1
**Status:** Ready for Design Implementation

---

## 1. Executive Summary

This brief addresses the addition of a **Savings Goals card** to the Dashboard's left column, positioned below the existing Category Breakdown (donut chart) card. The card presents three savings goals — Emergency Fund (75%), Vacation (40%), and New Laptop (90%) — each with a labeled progress bar, current/target amounts, and contextual status signals.

The feature extends the dashboard's core value proposition: giving users a real-time, glanceable financial health snapshot. Savings goals shift the product from purely reactive expense tracking toward proactive financial planning.

---

## 2. User Needs Analysis

### 2.1 Primary User Goals

| User Goal | Priority | Insight |
|-----------|----------|---------|
| Know how close I am to each savings target | High | Users need progress framing (% and absolute amounts), not just a number |
| Identify which goal needs attention | High | Visual hierarchy and color signals should surface urgency at a glance |
| Feel motivated to continue saving | Medium | Near-complete goals (90%) should feel rewarding; low-progress goals (40%) should feel achievable, not discouraging |
| Understand context without deep interaction | High | The card must be scannable in under 5 seconds — no clicks required for core insight |

### 2.2 Secondary User Goals

- Compare relative priority across goals simultaneously
- Trust that the data reflects real-time state (ties to the dashboard's reactive update model)
- Access an edit/manage flow for goals without disrupting the dashboard context

### 2.3 User Mental Models

Users approaching a savings goals widget typically think in two frames:

1. **Milestone thinking** — "How far have I come?" → Favor progress bars with percentage labels
2. **Gap thinking** — "How much more do I need?" → Favor remaining amount display alongside the bar

Both models must be supported. The design should show **percentage completion** (milestone) and **remaining amount** (gap) for each goal simultaneously.

---

## 3. Information Architecture

### 3.1 Card Hierarchy

```
Savings Goals Card
├── Card Header
│   ├── Title: "Savings Goals"
│   └── Action: "Manage" link (secondary, right-aligned)
├── Goal Row — Emergency Fund
│   ├── Goal name + status icon
│   ├── Progress bar (75%, emerald-500 fill)
│   └── Amount row: "$7,500 of $10,000 · $2,500 to go"
├── Goal Row — Vacation
│   ├── Goal name + status icon
│   ├── Progress bar (40%, blue-600 fill)
│   └── Amount row: "$1,200 of $3,000 · $1,800 to go"
└── Goal Row — New Laptop
    ├── Goal name + status icon
    ├── Progress bar (90%, amber-500 fill — near complete)
    └── Amount row: "$1,800 of $2,000 · $200 to go"
```

### 3.2 Data Points Per Goal

Each goal row must surface exactly these data points, in priority order:

1. **Goal name** — identity anchor
2. **Progress percentage** — fastest comprehension signal
3. **Progress bar** — spatial/proportional encoding
4. **Saved amount** — current state ("$7,500")
5. **Target amount** — context ("of $10,000")
6. **Remaining amount** — gap framing ("$2,500 to go")
7. **Status signal** — color-coded urgency or celebration

### 3.3 Left Column Stack Order (Post-Addition)

```
Left Column (360px)
├── 1. Budget Summary Card
├── 2. Category Breakdown Card (donut chart)
└── 3. Savings Goals Card  ← NEW
```

The left column will grow taller. Verify that on 768–1024px breakpoints, the stacked single-column layout remains scrollable and does not push the expense list below the fold excessively.

---

## 4. Visual Design Specification

### 4.1 Card Container

| Property | Value |
|----------|-------|
| Background | `surface-primary` (`#1E293B`) |
| Border radius | 16px |
| Shadow | `md` (`0 4px 16px rgba(0,0,0,0.4)`) |
| Padding | 24px |
| Gap between goal rows | 20px |
| Width | 360px (inherits left column) |

### 4.2 Card Header

- **Left:** "Savings Goals" — `heading-2` (16px, 600 weight), `text-primary` color
- **Right:** "Manage" — `label` (12px, 500 weight), `cta-primary` (`gold-amber`) color, text link style with no underline default, underline on hover
- **Layout:** `row`, `align: center`, `justify: space-between`

### 4.3 Goal Row Structure

Each goal row is a `column` container with 8px internal gap.

#### 4.3.1 Goal Row — Top Line

`row`, `align: center`, `justify: space-between`

- **Left:** Goal name — `body` (14px, 400 weight), `text-primary`
- **Right:** Percentage — `label` (12px, 500 weight), color varies by status (see 4.5)

#### 4.3.2 Progress Bar

| Property | Value |
|----------|-------|
| Height | 8px |
| Width | fill (full card inner width) |
| Track background | `surface-elevated` (`#293548`) |
| Track radius | 4px |
| Fill radius | 4px |
| Fill color | Status-dependent (see 4.5) |
| Fill width | Proportional to completion % |

#### 4.3.3 Goal Row — Bottom Line

`row`, `align: center`, `justify: space-between`

- **Left:** Saved amount — `label` (12px, 500), `text-secondary`
  - Format: `"$7,500 saved"`
- **Right:** Remaining — `label` (12px, 500), `text-secondary`
  - Format: `"$2,500 to go"` — or **`"Almost there!"`** in `cta-primary` color when ≥ 90%

### 4.4 Goal Data Values

| Goal | Saved | Target | Remaining | Percentage |
|------|-------|--------|-----------|------------|
| Emergency Fund | $7,500 | $10,000 | $2,500 | 75% |
| Vacation | $1,200 | $3,000 | $1,800 | 40% |
| New Laptop | $1,800 | $2,000 | $200 | 90% |

### 4.5 Status Color System

The dashboard's dark theme uses the existing design token system. Map goal completion states to tokens:

| State | Completion Range | Bar Fill Color | Percentage Text Color | Notes |
|-------|-----------------|----------------|----------------------|-------|
| Early progress | 0–49% | `info` (`#38BDF8`) | `text-secondary` | Neutral, encouraging |
| Good progress | 50–79% | `success` (`teal-success` `#14B8A6`) | `success` | Positive momentum |
| Near complete | 80–99% | `warning` (`gold-amber` `#F59E0B`) | `warning` | Urgency + excitement |
| Complete | 100% | `success` | `success` | Celebration state |

**Applied to the three goals:**

| Goal | % | Bar Color | Label Color |
|------|---|-----------|-------------|
| Emergency Fund | 75% | `success` (teal) | `success` |
| Vacation | 40% | `info` (blue) | `text-secondary` |
| New Laptop | 90% | `warning` (amber) | `warning` |

> **Rationale:** Color alone never carries meaning — percentage text and amount labels provide redundant encoding for accessibility compliance (WCAG AA).

### 4.6 Dividers Between Goal Rows

A 1px horizontal divider (`border-default` color, `slate-border` `#334155`) separates each goal row. Height: 1px, width: fill.

---

## 5. Interaction Patterns

### 5.1 Interaction Matrix

| Element | Trigger | Behavior |
|---------|---------|----------|
| "Manage" link | Click | Opens a settings-style modal or navigates to a Goals management view (outside current scope — link is present but target TBD) |
| Goal row | Hover | Row background shifts to `surface-elevated` (`#293548`), 150ms ease-out. Cursor: pointer. |
| Goal row | Click | Reserved for future goal detail popover (consistent with expense row pattern). For now: no-op or disabled pointer. Document as future affordance. |
| Progress bar fill | Load / month change | Animates from 0% to target width, 600ms ease-out. Consistent with existing chart animation timing. |
| Percentage text | Load / month change | Counter-animates from 0 to final value, 400ms ease-out. Consistent with amount counter behavior. |

### 5.2 Hover State Detail

On goal row hover, apply:
- Background: `surface-elevated` (`#293548`)
- Transition: `background-color 150ms ease-out`
- No shadow change required (contained within card)
- Progress bar does **not** change on row hover (data is factual, not interactive)

### 5.3 Popover Pattern (Future-Proofing)

The expense rows use a detail popover anchored to the right. If goal rows become clickable in a future iteration, follow the same popover pattern with:
- Goal name as header
- Contribution history list
- "Add contribution" CTA
- Edit/delete goal actions

Document this pattern now so the interaction model is consistent when implemented.

---

## 6. Accessibility Requirements

### 6.1 Color & Contrast

All text must meet WCAG AA contrast ratios against `surface-primary` (`#1E293B`):

| Text | Color | Background | Requirement |
|------|-------|------------|-------------|
| Goal name | `silver-text` `#CBD5E1` | `#1E293B` | ✅ Passes (≈ 9:1) |
| Percentage | `gold-amber` `#F59E0B` | `#1E293B` | ✅ Passes (≈ 7:1) |
| Amount labels | `muted-text` `#64748B` | `#1E293B` | ⚠️ Verify — may be borderline at 12px. Use `silver-text` fallback if needed. |

> **Action item:** Verify `muted-text` on `surface-primary` contrast ratio. If it fails at 12px/500 weight, elevate to `text-primary` (`silver-text`) for the amount sub-labels.

### 6.2 Non-Color Encoding

Progress bars must never rely on color alone:
- Percentage text provides numeric redundancy
- "Almost there!" text provides semantic redundancy for the 90% near-complete state
- Progress bar width provides spatial encoding independent of color

### 6.3 Keyboard Navigation

- The card header "Manage" link must be focusable (`Tab`) with a 2px `gold-amber` focus ring, 2px offset
- Goal rows, if made interactive, must receive focus with the same focus ring treatment
- Progress bars are decorative (`role="presentation"`) — the data is conveyed by adjacent text
- For screen readers, each goal row should be wrapped in a `role="group"` with `aria-label="[Goal Name] savings goal"`. The percentage and amounts should be readable in natural order.

### 6.4 Screen Reader Announcement

Each goal row should produce this logical read order:
> *"Emergency Fund savings goal. 75% complete. $7,500 saved of $10,000 target. $2,500 remaining."*

Achieved via DOM order matching visual order and no CSS-only reordering.

### 6.5 Reduced Motion

When `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce` is active:
- Progress bar fills instantly (no width animation)
- Percentage counter renders final value immediately
- Hover transitions are reduced to instant state changes (no 150ms transitions)

---

## 7. Responsive Behavior

### 7.1 Breakpoint Handling

| Breakpoint | Behavior |
|------------|----------|
| > 1440px | Left column: 400px. Card at full 400px width. Goal rows at full width. |
| 1024–1440px | Left column: 360px. Standard layout as specified. |
| 768–1024px | Left column stacks above right column. Card becomes full-width (up to 560px max). Goal row labels may need to truncate at 200px — use `text-overflow: ellipsis` on goal names. |
| < 768px | Out of scope per PRD (desktop only). |

### 7.2 Left Column Scroll Behavior

With the addition of three cards (Budget Summary, Category Breakdown, Savings Goals), the left column will likely exceed viewport height on 768–900px displays. Specify that the left column scrolls independently if its content overflows, using `overflow-y: auto` with a subtle scrollbar or scroll-shadow indicator at the bottom edge.

---

## 8. Empty & Edge States

### 8.1 No Goals Defined

If a user has no savings goals configured:
- Show a minimal empty state inside the card area: a small piggy bank or target icon (48px, `border-default` color) + `"No savings goals yet"` (`body`, `text-secondary`) + `"+ Add a goal"` text link (`label`, `cta-primary`)
- Do not hide the card entirely — the empty state itself serves as a discovery point

### 8.2 Goal Completed (100%)

- Progress bar shows full fill in `success` color
- Percentage label reads "Complete ✓" in `success` color
- Remaining amount line reads `"Goal reached! 🎉"` in `success` color
- Brief entrance animation on first completion: bar pulses once (scale 1.0 → 1.02 → 1.0, 300ms) — respects reduced motion setting

### 8.3 Goal Overcontributed (> 100%)

If contributions exceed the target (user input error or rounding):
- Cap progress bar at 100% fill visually
- Show `"Goal exceeded"` in `success` color
- Do not show a red/error state — overcontribution is positive

### 8.4 Very Long Goal Names

Goal names longer than ~20 characters will overflow at 360px card width. Apply:
- `white-space: nowrap`
- `overflow: hidden`
- `text-overflow: ellipsis`
- Full name exposed via native `title` attribute tooltip

---

## 9. Animation Specification

| Animation | Duration | Easing | Trigger |
|-----------|----------|--------|---------|
| Progress bar fill (load) | 600ms | `ease-out` | Card enters viewport |
| Progress bar fill (month change) | 600ms | `ease-out` | Month selector chevron click |
| Percentage counter | 400ms | `ease-out` | Same triggers as above |
| Goal row hover background | 150ms | `ease-out` | `mouseenter` |
| Goal row hover background exit | 150ms | `ease-out` | `mouseleave` |
| Completion pulse (100%) | 300ms | `ease-in-out` | First render at 100% state |
| Stagger between goal rows | 80ms offset | — | Each bar starts 80ms after previous |

> **Stagger rationale:** 80ms per row is slightly longer than the chart's 50ms per segment, appropriate since goals are larger visual units. Total stagger for 3 goals: 160ms, within the 600ms animation window.

---

## 10. Component Node Specification

The following node structure extends `currentDesignSpec` by adding the Savings Goals card as a sibling to `donut-chart-card` within `left-column` at `order: 2`.

### 10.1 Node Tree Outline

```
savings-goals-card (parent: left-column, order: 2)
├── savings-goals-header
│   ├── savings-goals-title
│   └── savings-goals-manage-link
├── goal-row-emergency
│   ├── goal-emergency-top
│   │   ├── goal-emergency-name
│   │   └── goal-emergency-pct
│   ├── goal-emergency-bar-track
│   │   └── goal-emergency-bar-fill
│   └── goal-emergency-bottom
│       ├── goal-emergency-saved
│       └── goal-emergency-remaining
├── goal-divider-1
├── goal-row-vacation
│   ├── goal-vacation-top
│   │   ├── goal-vacation-name
│   │   └── goal-vacation-pct
│   ├── goal-vacation-bar-track
│   │   └── goal-vacation-bar-fill
│   └── goal-vacation-bottom
│       ├── goal-vacation-saved
│       └── goal-vacation-remaining
├── goal-divider-2
└── goal-row-laptop
    ├── goal-laptop-top
    │   ├── goal-laptop-name
    │   └── goal-laptop-pct
    ├── goal-laptop-bar-track
    │   └── goal-laptop-bar-fill
    └── goal-laptop-bottom
        ├── goal-laptop-saved
        └── goal-laptop-remaining
```

### 10.2 Key Node Properties

| Node ID | Type | Key Properties |
|---------|------|----------------|
| `savings-goals-card` | `section` | `background: surface-primary`, `radius: 16`, `shadow: md`, `px: 24`, `py: 24`, `gap: 20` |
| `savings-goals-title` | `text` | `content: "Savings Goals"`, `typography: heading-2`, `color: text-primary`, `weight: 600` |
| `savings-goals-manage-link` | `text` | `content: "Manage"`, `typography: label`, `color: cta-primary` |
| `goal-emergency-name` | `text` | `content: "Emergency Fund"`, `typography: body`, `color: text-primary` |
| `goal-emergency-pct` | `text` | `content: "75%"`, `typography: label`, `color: success` |
| `goal-emergency-bar-track` | `container` | `width: fill`, `height: 8`, `radius: 4`, `background: surface-elevated` |
| `goal-emergency-bar-fill` | `container` | `width: 75%`, `height: 8`, `radius: 4`, `background: success` |
| `goal-emergency-saved` | `text` | `content: "$7,500 saved"`, `typography: label`, `color: text-secondary` |
| `goal-emergency-remaining` | `text` | `content: "$2,500 to go"`, `typography: label`, `color: text-secondary` |
| `goal-vacation-pct` | `text` | `content: "40%"`, `typography: label`, `color: text-secondary` |
| `goal-vacation-bar-fill` | `container` | `width: 40%`, `background: info` |
| `goal-vacation-saved` | `text` | `content: "$1,200 saved"` |
| `goal-vacation-remaining` | `text` | `content: "$1,800 to go"` |
| `goal-laptop-pct` | `text` | `content: "90%"`, `typography: label`, `color: warning` |
| `goal-laptop-bar-fill` | `container` | `width: 90%`, `background: warning` |
| `goal-laptop-saved` | `text` | `content: "$1,800 saved"` |
| `goal-laptop-remaining` | `text` | `content: "Almost there!"`, `color: warning` |
| `goal-divider-1` | `divider` | `width: fill`, `height: 1`, `background: border-default` |
| `goal-divider-2` | `divider` | `width: fill`, `height: 1`, `background: border-default` |

---

## 11. Design Checklist

Prior to implementation sign-off, verify:

- [ ] Card appears at `left-column` `order: 2`, below `donut-chart-card`
- [ ] All three goals render with correct percentages: 75%, 40%, 90%
- [ ] Progress bar fills match percentage widths proportionally
- [ ] Color coding matches: teal (75%), blue (40%), amber (90%)
- [ ] "Almost there!" renders in `warning` color for the 90% goal
- [ ] "Manage" link is present and visually distinct in `cta-primary` color
- [ ] Dividers separate goal rows cleanly
- [ ] Progress bar animations trigger on card load (600ms, staggered 80ms)
- [ ] Hover state on goal rows applies `surface-elevated` background
- [ ] Left column total height does not break dashboard two-column alignment
- [ ] Contrast ratios verified for all text/background combinations
- [ ] Reduced motion variant renders bars at full width instantly
- [ ] Screen reader read order is logical for each goal row
- [ ] Focus ring present on "Manage" link

---

*Brief produced by UX Research Agent | CashPulse v1.1 | Dashboard Screen*
